summary: Hotz warns that talented tech workers are unwittingly building a 'neofeudal' future where AI and automation will marginalize all labor, making their accumulated wealth worthless. He argues that in a world where capital is the only force, advanced AI systems will inevitably separate workers from whatever they've saved through advertising, scams, or government lobbying. Unlike historical feudalism where peasants had value as labor, the machine-driven future offers no such protection. His solution: refuse to participate in building these systems.
key_points: Money accumulated now won't protect you when AI can outmaneuver humans at every turn—GPT$$$ will find ways to extract whatever you have
Historical feudalism needed peasants for labor; neofeudalism won't, eliminating any leverage workers currently have
The neofeudal world won't operate on capitalist principles, so capitalist strategies like accumulating assets won't work
Working at large tech companies actively accelerates the system that will eventually consume the workers building it
The only solution is collective non-participation—individual escape is impossible
money_quotes: In the future, when labor is fully marginalized and capital is the only force, you will not be able to afford GPT$$$ (it's $1B per month), only the billionaires will.
GPT$$$ is surely smart enough to separate you from whatever you have, be that with targeting advertising, a scam you fall for, or lobbying your government to take it from you.
A pile of money will buy you nothing in the neofeudal world.
When the grain is produced by machines, the peasants are cut out of the loop.
They will find a way to make whatever you have worthless.
If you work at a large company... you are actively bringing about the system that will kill you.
We will either all be in the underclass together or not.
key_insight: Tech workers building AI systems are sawing off the branch they're sitting on—no amount of personal wealth accumulation will protect them from the labor-marginalizing future they're creating.
